<<vegetable>> <<Jamul Indian Village>> <<is known>> <<for its>> <<rich>> <<cultural>> <<heritage>> <<and>> <<strong>> <<sense>> <<of>> <<community>>.<<

<<vegetable>> <<The>> <<village>> <<is>> <<planning>> <<to>> <<build>> <<a>> <<new>> <<community>> <<center>> <<with>> <<a>> <<unique>> <<design>> <<The>> <<center>> <<will>> <<be>> <<constructed>> <<in>> <<the>> <<shape>> <<of>> <<a>> <<regular>> <<hexagon>> <<with>> <<each>> <<side>> <<measuring>> <<30>> <<meters>>.<<

<<vegetable>> <<The>> <<community>> <<also>> <<decided>> <<to>> <<incorporate>> <<a>> <<circular>> <<garden>> <<in>> <<the>> <<center>> <<of>> <<the>> <<hexagon>> <<with>> <<the>> <<garden's>> <<circumference>> <<touching>> <<the>> <<midpoints>> <<of>> <<the>> <<hexagon's>> <<sides>>.<<

<<vegetable>> <<Calculate>> <<the>> <<area>> <<of>> <<the>> <<hexagonal>> <<community>> <<center>>.<<

<<vegetable>> <<The>> <<area>> <<of>> <<a>> <<regular>> <<hexagon>> <<can>> <<be>> <<calculated>> <<using>> <<the>> <<formula>> <<A = (3√3/2) * s²,<<

<<vegetable>> <<where>> <<s>> <<is>> <<the>> <<side>> <<length>> <<of>> <<the>> <<hexagon>>.<<

<<vegetable>> <<Substituting>> <<s = 30>> <<meters>> <<into>> <<the>> <<formula>> <<gives>> <<A = (3√3/2) * (30)² = (3√3/2) * 900 = 1350√3 ≈ 2338.27 square meters.<<
